---
title: "Figure 2 — Parameter recovery: the means agree, the covariance does not"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
PUBLICATION figure 2 (NEW — there was no prior script for this one).
Question: across the five macro likelihoods, do the estimated parameter
covariances match the empirical spread of the fits, or do the classical methods
keep the mean right while wrecking the covariance?

Built ENTIRELY from the figure_3 grid on disk (data/433ed13), one representative
cell. Three covariance ellipses per algorithm in (log10 k_on, log10 k_off) space:

  - EMPIRICAL  : cov() of the MLE cloud           <- from *_mle_cloud_runs.csv
  - FISHER     : the likelihood's self-claimed cov <- Likelihood_Fisher_Covariance
  - CORRECTED  : the White sandwich H^-1 J H^-1    <- Likelihood_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance

ANCHOR: the model covariances (Fisher, corrected) are read from the *_battery_pool
files, i.e. evaluated at theta_pool (the misspecified MLE's probability limit),
NOT at theta_sim. The MLE cloud is centred at theta_pool, so that is the point
where its curvature and score variance are defined; anchoring the sandwich at
theta_sim makes corrected != empirical for the misspecified algorithms (R, MR).
With the theta_pool anchor, corrected ~ empirical for ALL five algorithms while
the naive Fisher under-states the spread (catastrophically for NR/NMR).

The cloud lives in GROUP units (each MLE pools `REP_GROUP` recordings), while the
battery covariances are per-recording. They are put on the same footing with
cov_scale = 1/REP_GROUP (this single constant is the only scaling assumption; for
a well-specified algorithm (IR) the three ellipses coincide, the built-in check).
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r params}
DATA_DIR  <- "data/433ed13"
REP_NCH      <- 100        # representative channel count
REP_NSIM     <- 10000      # recordings simulated — USE the 10000-replica runs, NOT the nsim_1000 ones
REP_NOISE    <- "0.1"      # noise_in_conductance_tau label (= current_noise 1e-4)
REP_GROUP    <- 10         # recordings pooled per MLE -> cloud point  (group_size)
REP_INTERVAL <- 0.1        # representative Delta/tau_min  (one of 0.01..1)

ALGOS  <- c("macro_NR","macro_NMR","macro_R","macro_MR","macro_IR")
ALGO_LAB <- c(macro_NR="NR", macro_NMR="NMR", macro_R="R", macro_MR="MR", macro_IR="IR")
ALGO_COL <- c(NR="#D55E00", NMR="#E69F00", R="#CC79A7", MR="#0072B2", IR="#009E73")

# the two kinetic parameters that carry the story (param_index 0 and 1)
PARAMS <- c("on","off")

# theta_sim truth in optimizer space = log10(natural): on=10, off=100
TRUTH <- c(on = log10(10), off = log10(100))

cov_scale <- 1 / REP_GROUP   # per-recording battery cov -> group-of-REP_GROUP units
```

```{r read}
read_skip1 <- function(path) read.csv(path, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

base <- file.path(DATA_DIR,
  sprintf("figure_3_nch_%d_nsim_%d_%s_noise_%s", REP_NCH, REP_NSIM, ALGOS, REP_NOISE))

# model covariances anchored at theta_pool (battery_pool), where the cloud lives
cloud   <- map_dfr(paste0(base, "_mle_cloud_runs.csv"), read_skip1)
battery <- map_dfr(paste0(base, "_battery_pool.csv"),   read_skip1) %>%
  mutate(component = str_replace(component_path, "^Probit_statistics_", ""))

# predicted (theoretical) bias = distortion-induced bias, defined at theta_sim,
# so it is read from battery_sim. Bias is intensive (no /group_size scaling).
bsim <- map_dfr(paste0(base, "_battery_sim.csv"), read_skip1) %>%
  mutate(component = str_replace(component_path, "^Probit_statistics_", ""),
         algo = factor(ALGO_LAB[algorithm], levels = unname(ALGO_LAB)))

near_int <- function(x) abs(x - REP_INTERVAL) < 1e-9
```

```{r cloud-points}
# raw MLE cloud in (on, off); one point per group fit (sample_index)
pts <- cloud %>%
  filter(component_path == "Model_Parameters_Hat", statistic == "value",
         Num_ch == REP_NCH, group_size == REP_GROUP, near_int(interval_in_tau),
         param_name %in% PARAMS) %>%
  select(algorithm, sample_index, param_name, value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = param_name, values_from = value) %>%
  filter(is.finite(on), is.finite(off)) %>%
  mutate(algo = factor(ALGO_LAB[algorithm], levels = unname(ALGO_LAB)))
```

```{r cov-helpers}
# assemble a 2x2 (on,off) block from a long (param_index, param_col, value) frame
mat2 <- function(df) {
  m <- matrix(NA_real_, 2, 2)
  for (i in 0:1) for (j in 0:1) {
    v <- df$value[df$param_index == i & df$param_col == j]
    if (length(v) == 1 && is.finite(v)) m[i + 1, j + 1] <- v
  }
  m
}

# 95% ellipse path for center (cx,cy) and 2x2 covariance S; NULL if not PD
ellipse_path <- function(cx, cy, S, level = 0.95, npoints = 200) {
  if (is.null(S) || any(!is.finite(S))) return(NULL)
  ev <- eigen(S, symmetric = TRUE)
  if (any(ev$values <= 0)) return(NULL)
  r  <- sqrt(qchisq(level, df = 2))
  th <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = npoints)
  Tm <- ev$vectors %*% diag(sqrt(ev$values)) %*% t(ev$vectors)
  P  <- Tm %*% (r * rbind(cos(th), sin(th)))
  data.frame(x = cx + P[1, ], y = cy + P[2, ])
}

# pull a battery covariance matrix (on,off block) for one algorithm
battery_cov <- function(comp, algo) {
  df <- battery %>%
    filter(component == comp, statistic == "value", probit == "mean",
           algorithm == algo, Num_ch == REP_NCH, near_int(interval_in_tau),
           param_index %in% 0:1, param_col %in% 0:1) %>%
    select(param_index, param_col, value)
  mat2(df) * cov_scale
}
```

```{r build-ellipses}
ell_rows <- list()
for (a in ALGOS) {
  lab <- ALGO_LAB[a]
  cl  <- pts %>% filter(algorithm == a)
  if (nrow(cl) < 3) next
  ctr <- c(mean(cl$on), mean(cl$off))

  Semp <- cov(cbind(cl$on, cl$off))                                   # empirical
  Sfis <- battery_cov("Likelihood_Fisher_Covariance", a)              # naive Fisher
  Scor <- battery_cov("Likelihood_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance", a)# sandwich

  for (nm in c("empirical","Fisher","corrected")) {
    S <- switch(nm, empirical = Semp, Fisher = Sfis, corrected = Scor)
    e <- ellipse_path(ctr[1], ctr[2], S)
    if (!is.null(e)) ell_rows[[length(ell_rows) + 1]] <-
      e %>% mutate(algo = lab, source = nm)
  }
}
ellipses <- bind_rows(ell_rows) %>%
  mutate(algo   = factor(algo, levels = unname(ALGO_LAB)),
         source = factor(source, levels = c("empirical","Fisher","corrected")))
```

```{r centers}
# three markers per panel: truth (theta_sim), empirical mean of the cloud
# (where the estimates actually land = empirical bias from truth), and the
# theory-predicted centre theta_sim + DIB (predicted bias).
emp_centers <- pts %>%
  group_by(algo) %>%
  summarise(on = mean(on), off = mean(off), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(kind = "empirical mean")

theo_centers <- bsim %>%
  filter(component == "Likelihood_Distortion_Induced_Bias", statistic == "value",
         probit == "mean", Num_ch == REP_NCH, near_int(interval_in_tau),
         param_name %in% c("on","off")) %>%
  select(algo, param_name, value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = param_name, values_from = value) %>%
  transmute(algo, on = unname(TRUTH["on"]) + on, off = unname(TRUTH["off"]) + off,
            kind = "predicted (truth+DIB)")

truth_center <- tibble(algo = factor(unname(ALGO_LAB), levels = unname(ALGO_LAB)),
                       on = unname(TRUTH["on"]), off = unname(TRUTH["off"]),
                       kind = "truth")

centers <- bind_rows(truth_center, emp_centers, theo_centers) %>%
  mutate(kind = factor(kind, levels = c("truth","empirical mean","predicted (truth+DIB)")))
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 14, fig.height = 3.6}
# axis window from the empirical clouds (robust quantiles) so a catastrophic NR
# ellipse runs off-panel rather than crushing the others
xr <- quantile(pts$on,  c(.005, .995), na.rm = TRUE)
yr <- quantile(pts$off, c(.005, .995), na.rm = TRUE)
pad <- c(-1, 1) * 0.15 * c(diff(xr), diff(yr))

g2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = pts, aes(on, off), colour = "grey55", size = .35, alpha = .18) +
  geom_path(data = ellipses, aes(x, y, colour = source, linetype = source),
            linewidth = .7) +
  geom_point(data = centers, aes(on, off, shape = kind),
             size = 2.4, stroke = .9, colour = "black", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ algo, nrow = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = xr + pad[1:2], ylim = yr + pad[1:2]) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(empirical = "black", Fisher = "#D55E00",
                                 corrected = "#0072B2"), name = NULL) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(empirical = "solid", Fisher = "dashed",
                                   corrected = "dotted"), name = NULL) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("truth" = 3, "empirical mean" = 19,
                                "predicted (truth+DIB)" = 1), name = NULL) +
  labs(x = expression(log[10]~k[on]), y = expression(log[10]~k[off]),
       title = sprintf("Parameter recovery at N_ch=%d, noise=%s, Delta/tau=%s, group=%d",
                       REP_NCH, REP_NOISE, REP_INTERVAL, REP_GROUP),
       subtitle = "Markers: + truth, filled = empirical mean, open = predicted (truth+DIB). Ellipses: empirical / Fisher / corrected (95%).") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
g2
ggsave("Figure_2.pdf", g2, width = 14, height = 3.6)
```

```{r diagnostics-table}
# numeric backup: per-algorithm ellipse areas (sqrt det) in each metric.
# For a well-specified algorithm empirical ~ Fisher ~ corrected.
area <- function(S) if (is.null(S) || any(!is.finite(S))) NA else
  { ev <- eigen(S, symmetric = TRUE)$values; if (any(ev <= 0)) NA else sqrt(prod(ev)) }
tibble(algorithm = ALGO_LAB[ALGOS]) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.character)) %>%
  bind_cols(map_dfr(ALGOS, function(a) {
    cl <- pts %>% filter(algorithm == a)
    tibble(
      empirical = area(if (nrow(cl) > 2) cov(cbind(cl$on, cl$off)) else NULL),
      Fisher    = area(battery_cov("Likelihood_Fisher_Covariance", a)),
      corrected = area(battery_cov("Likelihood_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance", a)))
  })) %>%
  mutate(Fisher_over_emp = as.numeric(Fisher) / as.numeric(empirical))
```
